ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-12-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Shustoff for John Gherini (Executor). Kevin Rodriguez for Catherine Beauclair (Beneficiary).

The Court indicated that the only issue raised by the Probate Examiner was whether the accounting fee should be considered as part of the statutory fees or not. The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to review of the order). A one year status on the reserve was set for September 9, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Of Objections To Executors First Account And Report, Etc Including Payment Of Attorneys Fees To Objectors Counsel
Off Calendar Dec-20-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Koplowitz and Mark Shustoff for and with John Gherini. Kevin Rodriguez for Cathi Beauclair.

The Court noted that Steven Jung was not present. Mr. Shustoff stated that Mr. Jung withdrew his objections. The Court advised Counsel that it received a letter from Pier Gherini indicating that he was not consulted by Mr. Jung regarding the settlement and provided copies of the letter to all Counsel. Mr. Shustoff is to provide a copy of said letter to Mr. Jung. Mr. Koplowitz stated that Elena Reis was in support of the settlement, adressed the issue of extraordinary fees, the issue of whether his fees are subject to Court approval and the issue of his fees being reviewed in camera. All Counsel are fine with in camera review of Mr. Koplowitz' fee request. Mr. Koplowitz also argued that Mr. Gherini does not express objection to the settlement with regard to Cathi or Northern Trust, that the settlement was a benefit to Mr. Gherini and that he lacks standing. The Petition for Order Approving Settlement, etc. and the accounting petition were taken under submission. Mr. Kolplowitz is to submit a proposed order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Benton.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-20-2012 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-05-2012 Continued To Dec-11-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-19-2012 Continued To Nov-28-12 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Shustoff and Edward Koplowitz for and with John Gherini. Steven Braccini and Kevin Rodriguez for Catherine Beauclair (Beneficiary). Steven Jung appeared by telephone for Northern Trust Co.

The Court noted that the declaration by Thomas makes a number of references to Mr. Allen (Accountant who is now deceased) and disclosed that she had an acquaintance with Mr. Allen and his colleague Mr. Conahan, that Mr. Conahan had been her Accountant and that this does not affect her ability to be fair and impartial to any Party or Counsel. All Counsel elected to proceed. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether the Probate Court has jurisdiction to look at the action taken by John Gherini as Executor naming himself and his Brother as Directors of the Corporation and the amount of fees paid to John Gherini as Director. Counsel agreed to payment of the statutory fees for the Executor and Counsel. The Court bifurcated the issue of said fees and granted the request. Mr. Koplowitz is to prepare the order. The Court provided its thoughts and observations, and asked the Parties to step out of the courtroom and discuss the matter. Upon returning, Counsel advised the Court that they agreed to do mediation (private mediation as the matter does not qualify for Pro Bono Mediation). Mediation is to be completed by November 14, 2012. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by November 21, 2012. The remaining issues in the accounting petition were continued to November 28, 2012 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-22-2012 Continued To Sep-19-12 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Shustoff and Edward Koplowitz for and with John Gherini. Steven Braccini and Kevin Rodriguez for Catherine Beauclair (Beneficiary). Mary Casserly specially appearing for Steve Jong for Northern Trust Company.

The Court has question as to whether the Probate Court has jurisdiction to look at the transaction of sale of the corporate property. Mr. Koplowitz stated that sale of the corporate property was done by the Director in the corporation, that fees were paid by the corporation and that the numbers were provided in the accounting for transparency. Mr. Rodriguez explained actions taken by Tom Gherini and John Gherini, and stated that he thinks the probate Court does have jurisdiction to review those actions. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the request for statutory Attorney fees and statutory Executor fees. The Court will not approve the accounting at this point or rule on the Attorney fees or the Executor fees, and give Counsel the opportunity to brief the issue of this Court having jurisdiction over the Directors fees and jurisdiction to rule on actions of the Executor in the corporation. The Court will approve the second preliminary distribution. The accounting petition was continued to September 19, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Concurrent briefs with no sur-rebuttals are to be filed no later than September 12, 2012.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy C. Garcia.
